skill.name: contribute-catalog
skill.description: Author a new HyperFrames registry block (caption style, VFX block, transition, lower third) or component (text effect, overlay, snippet) and ship it as an upstream PR to the hyperframes repo. Use ONLY when the user wants to CONTRIBUTE to the public catalog.
skill.summary: Guide users from idea to merged PR for new HyperFrames registry blocks and components.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 136 static findings are false positives. The skill is legitimate documentation for contributing to the HyperFrames open-source video rendering framework. The flagged 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns are actually backticks within HTML/CSS/JS code blocks in markdown documentation, not shell commands. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference mulberry32, which is a non-cryptographic seeded PRNG used for reproducible visual effects. All hardcoded URLs are standard CDN endpoints (Google Fonts, jsdelivr, Three.js). No prompt injection attempts or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: External CLI commands referenced in workflow
low_findings.description: The skill documents usage of CLI tools (hyperframes, git, gh, npx) for linting, validation, and publishing. These are standard development tools, not security risks. The commands are well-documented and follow safe open-source contribution practices.

content.value_statement: Contributing a new video effect to HyperFrames requires navigating a multi-step process. This skill guides you from initial idea through to a merged PR, ensuring your block passes linting, validation, and preview checks.
actual_capabilities: Scaffold new registry blocks and components with correct directory structure and naming conventions
Generate HTML compositions for caption styles, VFX effects, and overlays using provided templates
Apply non-negotiable caption rules (font size, text-stroke, overflow handling, karaoke animation)
Create Three.js VFX blocks with seeded PRNG and GSAP-driven state proxy patterns
Run validation pipeline (lint, validate, render, snapshot, publish)
Prepare and submit pull requests with proper commit messages and preview links
limitations: Only covers contributing to the public HyperFrames catalog, not in-project authoring or installing existing items
Requires external contributors to attach MP4 previews; catalog PNG generation is internal-only
Does not generate video content itself; it only scaffolds the contribution workflow
Assumes user has a GitHub account and gh CLI configured

use_cases.title: Open-source contributor adding a caption style
use_cases.description: A developer wants to add a new viral-style caption animation to the HyperFrames public catalog. They use this skill to scaffold the block, apply the template, validate, and submit a PR.
use_cases.target_user: Open-source contributors to the HyperFrames project

use_cases.title: Agency designer shipping a branded VFX effect
use_cases.description: A motion designer at an agency builds a custom particle effect in Three.js. They use this skill to package it as a reusable registry block with proper metadata and documentation.

use_cases.title: Internal HeyGen engineer publishing a transition
use_cases.description: A HeyGen engineer creates a new wipe transition and uses this skill to follow the contribution workflow, run the internal upload script, and merge via the team pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Start a new caption block contribution
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to contribute a new caption style to the HyperFrames registry. The effect is a 'bounce-in word highlight' where each word scales up and changes color when spoken. Help me scaffold and build it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - starting a new contribution from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold a VFX Three.js block
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk me through creating a VFX block with a rotating wireframe globe using Three.js. I need the state proxy pattern and seeded PRNG. Use the naming prefix 'gl'.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and prepare for PR submission
prompt_templates.prompt: I've built a registry component called 'shimmer-sweep'. Run me through the full validation pipeline (lint, validate, render, snapshot, publish) and generate the PR description.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - finalizing a contribution for merge

prompt_templates.title: Fix a caption overflow issue
prompt_templates.prompt: My caption group is overflowing the 1920x1080 canvas. The text says 'Welcome to the future of automated content creation'. Apply the fitTextFontSize pattern and verify the font-size adjustment works.
prompt_templates.scenario: Debugging - fixing a specific technical issue

output_examples.input: I want to contribute a typewriter caption style with the prefix 'tw'.
output: Generates HTML with per-character <span> animation via GSAP timeline
Adds registry-item.json with type 'hyperframes:block' and correct dimensions
Provides step-by-step validation and PR submission commands

output_examples.input: Build a particle VFX block with 500 particles rotating around a center point.
output: Generates Three.js template with seeded mulberry32 PRNG
Includes state proxy (rotY, particleSpread, etc.) animated by GSAP

output_examples.input: My block passes lint but validate shows a contrast warning on the white text against the gray background.
output: Identifies the contrast issue source in the CSS
Suggests using -webkit-text-stroke: 3px with rgba(0,0,0,0.8) for readability
Recommends re-running with --no-contrast flag to confirm the fix path
Documents the rule in the quality gate checklist

best_practices: Always use a 2-3 letter unique prefix for all element IDs to avoid collisions in sub-compositions
Use gsap.timeline({ paused: true }) and render via tl.eventCallback onUpdate, never requestAnimationFrame
Call window.__hyperframes.fitTextFontSize() on every caption group to prevent overflow
anti_patterns: Using Math.random() or Date.now() in compositions - breaks seekability and reproducibility
Mixing tl.from(el, { opacity: 0 }) with tl.set(el, { opacity: 1 }) at the same position - the from clobbers the set
Using CSS animations for cursor or decorative elements - CSS animations are not seekable in video output

faq.question: What is the difference between a block and a component?
faq.answer: A block is a full standalone composition with fixed dimensions and duration (e.g., a caption style). A component is a reusable snippet without fixed dimensions that adapts to any composition size (e.g., a CSS text effect).

faq.question: Do I need a GitHub account to contribute?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a GitHub account to open a pull request. Sign up at github.com/signup, then run 'gh auth login' to configure the CLI.

faq.question: Why must I use a seeded PRNG instead of Math.random()?
faq.answer: Math.random() produces different values on each render, which breaks frame-accurate seeking. A seeded PRNG like mulberry32 ensures identical output every time the composition is rendered.

faq.question: What does the quality gate checklist require?
faq.answer: It requires: 0 lint errors, 0 console errors on validate, oxfmt --check passes, registry.json updated, catalog docs page generated, publish to hyperframes.dev completed, and a preview MP4 or catalog image attached.

faq.question: Can I use CSS animations for cursor blinking in a caption style?
faq.answer: No. CSS animations are not seekable and will not appear correctly in seekable video output. Use GSAP tl.set calls at computed intervals instead.

faq.question: What is the minimum font size for caption text?
faq.answer: 96px minimum for proportional fonts. 64-72px is acceptable for monospace fonts since wider characters need less size to maintain readability.
